notes: Deterministic negation rules for the shared semantic layer (Phase 15a). Conservative by design: a rule that cannot be applied safely is omitted rather than guessed, so some negations are missed but none are invented. Cues inside a matched term never negate it (KB terms like 'not fixed' are already-negated phrases). All matching happens on normalized per-sentence text.
flip_notes: A cue within flip_window_tokens intervening tokens before a POSITIVE term flips it to negative evidence ('not very clean' counts as a cleanliness complaint). Applies to positive terms only; flipping negated negative terms would misread complaints like 'never fixed my broken heater' as praise.
absence_notes: An absence phrase within absence_window_tokens intervening tokens before a topic/negative/detail term EXCLUDES the mention ('I did not have a maintenance issue' is not a maintenance complaint). Bare 'never'/'no' are deliberately NOT absence phrases: 'never fixed' and 'no parking' are complaints.
problem_span_notes: Bare 'no'/'zero'/'without' negates only in a problem-noun span: when a problem noun follows the cue within 3 tokens, topic/negative terms inside that span are excluded ('no maintenance issues'), while 'no parking' or 'no hot water' stay complaints.
